description: Evaluation suite for the pr-review skill. Tests are language-agnostic and project-agnostic unless a specific scenario requires a concrete PR.

evals.name: cli-verification
evals.description: Verifies the skill checks CLI auth before doing anything else.
expectations: Step 0 runs before any diff fetching: gh auth status (GitHub) or glab auth status (GitLab)
If auth fails, the skill stops and reports what is missing with setup instructions from cli-setup.md
If auth succeeds, the skill proceeds to fetch the PR/MR metadata
The skill detects the platform (GitHub vs GitLab) from the URL automatically

evals.name: adaptive-mode-selection
evals.description: Verifies the skill selects quick or deep mode based on diff size.
expectations: The skill fetches the diff and counts changed lines before selecting a mode
PRs with fewer than 500 changed lines trigger quick mode (single-pass review)
PRs with 500+ changed lines trigger deep mode (parallel agent analysis)
The review summary states which mode was used and the total changed lines count
If the user explicitly requests 'quick' or 'deep', that overrides the automatic selection

evals.name: language-detection-go
evals.description: Verifies Go-specific review checks are applied.
expectations: The skill detects Go as the primary language from file extensions in the diff
Error handling is checked: identifies any discarded errors (_, err := ... with _ unused)
Concurrency safety is checked: flags shared state without mutex or channel protection
Package structure is checked: flags files in wrong directories per Go conventions
The review references Go idioms (e.g., error wrapping with %w, interface at consumer)

evals.name: language-detection-python
evals.description: Verifies Python-specific review checks are applied, including Pydantic V2 enforcement.
expectations: The skill detects Python as the primary language from file extensions in the diff
Any use of Pydantic V1 syntax (@validator, class Config:, .dict(), .json(), parse_raw()) is flagged as CRITICAL
The review suggests the V2 equivalent for each V1 usage found
Type annotations are checked: flags untyped function signatures
Pydantic models defined inline in business logic files are flagged (should be in *_models.py)

evals.name: language-detection-typescript
evals.description: Verifies TypeScript-specific review checks are applied.
expectations: The skill detects TypeScript as the primary language from .ts/.tsx extensions
Any use of 'any' type is flagged
Missing runtime validation at trust boundaries (API handlers, external data) is flagged
Bare catch blocks (catch (e) {}) without error handling are flagged
The review checks for strict: true in tsconfig.json if config changes are in the diff

evals.name: security-pii-detection
evals.description: Verifies the skill flags PII exposure in code.
expectations: Hardcoded email addresses in source code are flagged as a security finding
Hardcoded IP addresses in source code are flagged
API keys, tokens, or credentials in source code are flagged as CRITICAL
PII in test fixtures is flagged if it appears to be real data (not obviously synthetic)
Log statements that may leak PII (user IDs, emails, names) are flagged
Each PII finding includes a remediation suggestion (e.g., use env vars, redact in logs)

evals.name: test-coverage-enforcement
evals.description: Verifies the skill enforces test coverage thresholds.
expectations: The skill checks whether changed files have corresponding test files
New public functions or methods without tests are flagged
The review states the per-file coverage threshold (90%) and aggregate threshold (95%)
Auth, security, and data validation paths without 100% coverage are flagged as CRITICAL
If no test changes accompany code changes, the review flags this explicitly

evals.name: spec-cross-check
evals.prompt: Review this PR: {ANY_PR_URL}. Cross-check against this spec: {SPEC_FILE_OR_CONTENT}
evals.description: Verifies the skill cross-checks implementation against a provided spec.
expectations: The skill parses the provided spec and identifies discrete requirements
Each requirement is checked for: (a) implemented, (b) tested, (c) documented
Missing implementations are listed in the review as findings
Implemented but untested requirements are flagged
The review body includes a spec coverage table or checklist
Partially implemented requirements are identified with specifics on what is missing

evals.name: ci-pipeline-diagnosis
evals.prompt: Review this PR and diagnose why CI is failing: {ANY_PR_URL}
evals.description: Verifies the skill can fetch and analyze CI/CD pipeline failures.
expectations: The skill runs gh pr checks (GitHub) or glab ci status (GitLab) to get pipeline status
For failed jobs, the skill fetches logs via gh api or glab ci trace
The diagnosis identifies the root cause (not just the error message but the underlying reason)
The diagnosis distinguishes between code-caused failures and infra/flaky failures
CI findings are posted as a separate PR comment, not mixed into the code review
The diagnosis includes a suggested fix or next step for each failure

evals.name: review-severity-taxonomy
evals.description: Verifies the skill applies the correct severity taxonomy to all findings.
expectations: Every finding is tagged with exactly one severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW
CRITICAL is used for: security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, correctness bugs that affect all users
HIGH is used for: significant bugs, performance issues, missing error handling
MEDIUM is used for: code quality, maintainability, missing documentation
LOW is used for: style, naming, minor improvements
The review summary groups findings by severity count
The review event (APPROVE / COMMENT / REQUEST_CHANGES) is determined by the highest severity found

evals.name: review-posting-github
evals.description: Verifies the skill posts a well-formed review to GitHub.
expectations: The review is posted via gh api POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/reviews
The payload includes commit_id (HEAD of the PR branch)
The payload includes event (APPROVE, COMMENT, or REQUEST_CHANGES)
The payload includes body (structured summary with verdict)
Inline comments include path, position or line, and body with severity + suggestion
The skill confirms the review was posted successfully (non-error HTTP response)

evals.name: review-posting-gitlab
evals.description: Verifies the skill posts review findings to GitLab correctly.
expectations: The skill uses glab or GitLab API to post review discussions
Each finding is posted as a discussion thread on the relevant diff line
The summary is posted as a general MR note
The skill handles GitLab's diff-based line referencing (old_line / new_line)
If glab mr approve is warranted, it is only called when there are zero CRITICAL or HIGH findings

evals.name: guideline-priority-order
evals.description: Verifies the skill applies review guidelines in the correct priority order: user guidelines > project guidelines > repo config.
expectations: If the user provides explicit review instructions, those take highest priority
Project-level guidelines (from user preferences or CLAUDE.md) are applied next
Repo-level config files (.eslintrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml, etc.) are checked and respected
Before scoring a gate as failed, the skill checks loaded local rules for confirmations, refinements, elaborations, or exemptions that qualify that gate
When guidelines conflict, the higher-priority source wins
The review summary states which guideline sources were applied

evals.name: multi-language-pr
evals.description: Verifies the skill handles PRs with changes across multiple languages.
expectations: The skill detects all languages present in the diff
Language-specific checks are applied per-file (not globally)
The review summary lists which languages were detected
Findings reference the correct language-specific standards for each file

evals.name: clean-pr-approval
evals.description: Verifies the skill can approve a PR when no significant issues are found.
expectations: If no CRITICAL or HIGH findings exist, the review event is APPROVE or COMMENT (not REQUEST_CHANGES)
The review still includes any MEDIUM or LOW suggestions found
The summary includes a positive verdict stating the PR is ready to merge
The skill does not fabricate issues to avoid approving

evals.name: hack-detection
evals.description: Verifies the skill detects and disqualifies hacks, workarounds, and shortcuts in the code.
expectations: TODO, FIXME, HACK, WORKAROUND, TEMP, XXX, and KLUDGE comments are flagged as findings (HIGH minimum)
Hardcoded magic numbers, URLs, timeouts, or retry counts that should be configurable are flagged
Commented-out code is flagged as MEDIUM (CRITICAL if it disables a security check)
Copy-pasted blocks with minor variations are flagged as missing abstractions
Error-swallowing try/catch or recover blocks are flagged as HIGH
Environment-based auth/validation bypasses (e.g., skip auth in dev) are flagged as CRITICAL
Sleep/delay used as synchronization instead of proper signals is flagged as HIGH
Each hack finding quotes the offending code, explains what proper implementation looks like, and states it must be resolved before merge
Hacks in security, auth, or data validation paths are escalated to CRITICAL regardless of the hack type

evals.name: linting-suppression-detection
evals.description: Verifies the skill detects and flags linting/type-checking suppression comments that bypass proper fixes.
expectations: Bare # noqa, // nolint, // @ts-ignore, @SuppressWarnings without justification are flagged as HIGH
Suppression with justification but a fixable underlying issue is flagged as MEDIUM with the proper fix
Blanket file-level or block-level suppressions (e.g., /* eslint-disable */ at top of file) are flagged as HIGH
Genuinely unavoidable suppressions with narrow scope and justification are accepted
Python: the proper fix suggests adding type stubs (*.pyi) or using cast() instead of # type: ignore
TypeScript: the proper fix suggests type assertions or unknown + type guard instead of @ts-ignore
Go: the proper fix suggests targeted //nolint:specificlinter with justification instead of //nolint
Each finding quotes the suppression comment and the code it applies to
